extraction_notes_2d: In 2D: detect frames/canvases, sculptures, plinths; look for compositional centrality, scale, and lighting emphasis. CV hint: object detection for 'painting', 'sculpture', 'frame'; saliency maps to confirm prominence.
extraction_notes_3d: In 3D: check line-of-sight frequency to art objects from typical paths; prominence increases if art lies on primary circulation routes and has high visual exposure. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Artworks Prominent.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect text/sign panels; 'EXIT' is a strong cue; arrows/maps. CV hint: OCR + sign detection; classify signs as navigational vs branding by keywords (exit, restroom, gate, arrivals).
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: wayfinding nodes are labeled objects; evaluate density/visibility of signage along likely paths; in VR, signage should be readable at typical viewing distances. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Clear Exits / Wayfinding Signage.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: look for deep perspective, aligned openings, low occlusion. CV hint: depth estimation + occlusion rate; count visible floor area at distance; detect partition heights.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: compute visibility graphs / isovist area; high isovist area and long-range visibility along principal axes implies clear sightlines. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Clear Sightlines.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect text/logos; OCR keywords; classify sign as marketing. CV hint: logo detection + OCR; saliency to judge prominence.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: branding prominence increases if visible from main entry and along primary circulation; measure exposure frequency. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Commercial Branding.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect repeated doorways/walls; many bounded regions in layout; occlusions at eye level. CV hint: wall/door detection; count enclosed regions (scene graph).
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: build navigable cell decomposition; high number of rooms/cells per area suggests compartmentalization; compare to open-space volume ratio. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Compartmentalized Plan.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: look for many dominant line orientations, skewed vanishing points, multiple plane breaks. CV hint: line segment orientation entropy; plane segmentation count.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: compute surface normal diversity and curvature/planarity segmentation; high count of distinct planes/normal clusters implies complex geometry. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Complex Geometry.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: compute dominant hue distribution; cool palette if mean hue falls in blue-green range and saturation is non-trivial. Watch for white balance issues. CV hint: histogram in HSV; compare cool vs warm pixel ratio.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: use surface material colors / light temperature settings; aggregate dominant hue across major surfaces in the viewing volume. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Cool Color Palette.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: estimate illuminant from white surfaces; if whites/greys skew blue and skin tones cool, tag. CV hint: color constancy/illuminant estimation; compare correlated color temperature proxy from RGB ratios.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: use light sources’ CCT metadata if available; otherwise infer from rendering parameters; tag if dominant lights exceed threshold CCT. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Cool Lighting (High CCT).

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect people count and estimate free-space; crowding if people/object occupancy fraction is high in navigable region. CV hint: person detection + floor free-space segmentation; compute density metrics.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: compute navigable free-space volume and obstacle density; in VR, crowding can be derived from agent density + collision constraints. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Crowded Space.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect arcs/curves in dominant edges; curvature at architectural scale (walls/ceilings), not just objects. CV hint: edge detection + curve fitting; count long curved segments.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: compute surface curvature (mean curvature) on large surfaces; high proportion of curved surfaces implies curvilinear forms. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Curvilinear Forms.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: look for high luminance near apertures, daylight color cues, exterior view brightness; detect sun patches. CV hint: luminance gradient from window regions; exterior/interior contrast.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: use time-of-day/sun position; evaluate direct and indirect daylight contribution in lighting simulation; tag if daylight contributes materially to illuminance. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Daylighting.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect multi-hue lighting sources and colored light cast; look for LED strip geometry and saturated color pools. CV hint: identify saturated colored regions with lighting-like falloff + detect strip fixtures.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: tag fixtures with dynamic capability; if scene includes controllable light groups or time-varying lighting programs, mark dynamic_lighting. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Dynamic Lighting.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: object detection for fans; look for radial blades/guards. CV hint: fan classifier or general object detector categories 'fan/ceiling_fan'.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: fixture metadata; count fan objects in the scene graph; tag if within typical field of view from occupied zones. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Fans Visible.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: look for repeated motifs at different sizes; optionally estimate fractal dimension or scale-invariance across spatial frequencies. CV hint: multi-scale texture descriptors; power spectrum ~1/f-like patterns can support (but not prove) the tag.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: analyze patterns on surfaces at multiple LODs; compute scale-invariance metrics on texture/geometry; tag when repetition across scales is present in geometry or textures. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Fractal-Like Pattern.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect saturated pixels/hotspots, high local contrast, visible bright sources; look for reflection patterns. CV hint: highlight detection + glare probability models (e.g., identify large saturated regions with surrounding halo).
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: evaluate glare metrics (UGR/DFG) via lighting simulation; tag if predicted glare exceeds threshold at typical viewpoints. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Glare Present.

extraction_notes_2d: 2D: detect large transparent/reflective planar surfaces with window frames; reflections and refraction cues. CV hint: semantic segmentation for 'window/glass'; estimate proportion of glass pixels.
extraction_notes_3d: 3D: material properties marked as glass; compute visible surface area proportion for glass surfaces from typical viewpoints. From 3D or scene data, use geometry/material cues for Glass Prominent.
